current.title: 読みのストラテジー0 名詞修飾 Noun modification めしうよ Sentences that contain noun-modifying clauses are much easier to decipher if you're able to accurately parse their structure. The key here is to understand where the noun-modifying clause starts and ends. ストラテジー Noun-modifying clauses always come before the modified noun, and their predicate is in the plain form. If you find a verb right before a noun, it is very likely the end of a noun-modifying clause. The subject of a noun-modifying clause is typically marked with が rather than は, so look for a が that might help you figure out where the noun-modifying clause starts.

Once you think you've found the referent, test it by seeing whether the sentence makes sense when that word or phrase is used in place of the demonstrative word. contentBlocks.title: Lesson 1 Overview: Famous People and New Encounters
contentBlocks.text: Lesson 1 is organized around reading and writing about famous or respected people, then practicing introductions, small talk, and listening about international students in Japan.

contentBlocks.title: Bessatsu Vocabulary Reference
contentBlocks.text: Primary vocabulary source for Quartet I Lesson 1, used as the unlock source for lesson cards.

contentBlocks.title: Bessatsu Kanji Reference
contentBlocks.text: Primary kanji reference pages for Quartet I Lesson 1 vocabulary and furigana validation.

contentBlocks.title: Reading 1 Prep and Follow-up: Hayao Miyazaki
contentBlocks.text: Pre-reading and post-reading prompts for discussing Japanese animation, Hayao Miyazaki, and what students learned from the reading.

contentBlocks.title: Reading 1: Hayao Miyazaki
contentBlocks.text: Main reading about Hayao Miyazaki as a representative Japanese animated-film director, including his works, work style, and artistic standards.

contentBlocks.title: Reading 2 Prep and Follow-up: Shinya Yamanaka
contentBlocks.text: Pre-reading and post-reading prompts for discussing Nobel Prize winners, respected people, iPS cells, and research goals.

contentBlocks.title: Reading 2: Shinya Yamanaka
contentBlocks.text: Main reading about researcher Shinya Yamanaka, iPS cells, his background, and the motivation behind his research.

contentBlocks.title: Reading Strategy: Noun Modification
contentBlocks.text: Reading strategy practice for identifying noun-modifying clauses and their subjects in longer reading passages.

contentBlocks.title: Reading Strategy: Demonstrative References
contentBlocks.text: Reading strategy practice for identifying what demonstratives such as これ, それ, and あれ refer to in context.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar Note: Nといえば
contentBlocks.text: Introduces Nといえば for bringing up the association that comes to mind or shifting to a related topic.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar Note: ~なら
contentBlocks.text: Explains conditional/topic uses of なら for advice, requests, topic pickup, and contrast.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar Note: Nなら Uses
contentBlocks.text: Continues Nなら examples for quoted topics and contrastive statements.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar Note: ~とおり(に) / Nどおり(に)
contentBlocks.text: Explains using とおり and どおり to describe actions or results that follow instructions, plans, statements, or expectations.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar Note: ~らしい Hearsay
contentBlocks.text: Introduces らしい for reporting information learned indirectly or inferred from another source.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar Note: Nらしい Typicality
contentBlocks.text: Explains Nらしい for describing characteristics that are typical of a person, season, group, or category.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar Note: ~ために
contentBlocks.text: Explains ために for purpose clauses and intentional actions done in order to achieve something.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar Note: ~ためのN
contentBlocks.text: Explains ための before nouns to describe intended purpose or use.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar Note: ~がきっかけで
contentBlocks.text: Explains how to mark an event or experience as the trigger that led to a later action or change.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar Note: ~ようになる
contentBlocks.text: Explains changes in ability, habit, or state with ようになる.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar Note: ~ようにする
contentBlocks.text: Explains making an effort or habit to do something with ようにする.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar Note: Nによると
contentBlocks.text: Explains によると for identifying the source of reported information, often with そうだ or らしい.

contentBlocks.title: Writing: Model Profile of a Respected Famous Person
contentBlocks.text: Model composition and structure for introducing a respected famous person, including background, achievements, and reasons for respect.

contentBlocks.title: Writing Task: A Famous Person I Respect
contentBlocks.text: Planning prompts, writing points, and final composition task for writing 300-400 characters about a famous person the learner respects.

contentBlocks.title: Speaking Conversation 1: First Meeting
contentBlocks.text: Preparation, listening check, and setup for introducing yourself to a new roommate or teacher and giving a small present.

contentBlocks.title: Model Dialogue: New Roommate
contentBlocks.text: Model casual conversation for meeting a new roommate, giving a self-introduction, asking what to call someone, and continuing the exchange.

contentBlocks.title: Speaking Practice: Self-Introduction Flow
contentBlocks.text: Flowchart and role-play practice for greeting, self-introduction, questions, small presents, and formal conversation challenge.

contentBlocks.title: Speaking Conversation 2: Small Talk with Friends
contentBlocks.text: Preparation and listening check for starting casual conversation before class and changing topics naturally.

contentBlocks.title: Model Dialogue: Before Class
contentBlocks.text: Model casual conversation for greeting a friend, talking about weather, asking about the weekend, and asking follow-up questions.

contentBlocks.title: Speaking Practice: Keeping Conversation Going
contentBlocks.text: Flowchart and practice patterns for beginning with weather, changing topics, commenting, and asking for details.

contentBlocks.title: Listening 1: Ghibli Museum
contentBlocks.text: Listening task about international students discussing the Ghibli Museum, including content-check questions and detail prompts.

contentBlocks.title: Listening 2: Store Greetings
contentBlocks.text: Listening task about an American exchange student discussing greetings from store clerks in Japan.

exercises.label: Workbook Reading 1 A: True/False Check
exercises.prompt: Check whether each statement matches Reading 1 about Hayao Miyazaki.

exercises.label: Workbook Reading 1 B: Reading Strategy Practice
exercises.prompt: Practice noun modification and demonstrative reference using sentences from Reading 1.

exercises.label: Workbook Reading 1 C: Content Questions
exercises.prompt: Answer comprehension questions about Reading 1, including subjects, reasons, and profile details.

exercises.label: Workbook Reading 2 A: True/False Check
exercises.prompt: Check whether each statement matches Reading 2 about Shinya Yamanaka.

exercises.label: Workbook Reading 2 B: Reading Strategy Practice
exercises.prompt: Practice noun modification and demonstrative reference using sentences from Reading 2.

exercises.label: Workbook Reading 2 C: Content Questions
exercises.prompt: Answer comprehension questions about Reading 2, including reasons, habits, goals, and research motivations.

exercises.label: Workbook Grammar A: ~とおり(に) and ~らしい
exercises.prompt: Complete sentences and conversations using ~とおり(に), Nどおり(に), and ~らしい.

exercises.label: Workbook Grammar A Continued: ~とおり(に) and ~らしい
exercises.prompt: Continue completing practice items for ~とおり(に), Nどおり(に), and ~らしい.

exercises.label: Workbook Grammar A: ~ために, ~ためのN, and ~ようになる
exercises.prompt: Complete sentences and conversations using purpose expressions and change-of-state expressions.

exercises.label: Workbook Grammar A: ~ようにする
exercises.prompt: Complete sentences and conversations using ~ようにする.

exercises.label: Workbook Grammar B: Summary Gap Fill
exercises.prompt: Use the lesson grammar expressions to complete sentences and conversations; each expression is used once.

exercises.label: Workbook Grammar B Continued: Summary Gap Fill
exercises.prompt: Continue the summary practice using the lesson grammar expressions.

exercises.label: Workbook Grammar B: Self-Introduction Writing
exercises.prompt: Write a self-introduction using at least three Lesson 1 grammar expressions and underline the expressions used.

exercises.label: Workbook Grammar C: Oral Practice
exercises.prompt: Answer oral prompts using the specified Lesson 1 grammar expressions.
